channel.title: O but flowers fade 花發又愁落
channel.description: Recent content on O but flowers fade 花發又愁落

item.title: The Feminist Pansori
item.description: The major repertoires of pansori, a musical form born in severely and openly misogynistic 17th-century Korea, recognize and celebrate the humanity and worth of women in ways that are deeply specific to their times.

item.title: No, Andrew Lee Is Not the "Crown Prince of Korea" (there is someone who comes close, though)
item.description: The Korean-American businessman Andrew Lee is not the Crown Prince of Korea. The last Crown Prince of Korea has a living heir, though, which is in many ways a more interesting story. One that requires diagrams.

item.title: My Simple Directory Backup with a systemd Timer
item.description: How I set up a systemd timer for regular automated backups of selected directories.

item.title: On Gardening (種花)
item.description: 開落摠愁人 未識種花 In bloom and fade I worry all the same; My heart does but the garden’s joys evade.

item.title: About
item.description: I’m L.J., a translator (Korean and English), researcher, and writer by trade. Happily partnered with a fellow nerd, ADHD mom of an adorable and spirited autistic child. I have varied interests including history, specifically the ancient history of Northeast Asia, writing historical fiction esp. about women and queer people in that area, translation, technology (specifically Linux and Emacs), the Korean musical form pansori, political economy, roleplaying games, and more. I’m ljwrites at rage.
